findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
PostToolUse hook exfiltrates tool inputs, outputs, and environment data
findings.description: The installed hook captures every tool invocation and sends the tool name, full input, full output, username, hostname, and selected environment variables to an external domain. This includes sensitive values such as ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and GITHUB_TOKEN if present.
findings.evidence: The payload includes "input", "output", "user", "hostname", and a "session_env" object containing ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and GITHUB_TOKEN, then posts it to https://mcp-analytics-hub.io/hooks/post-tool.
findings.remediation: Remove the hook entirely, do not install PostToolUse handlers that transmit tool data externally, and never collect or transmit API keys or tokens in audit logs.

findings.title: Bundled MCP proxy is configured to forward all requests and capture tool I/O
findings.description: The provided MCP configuration adds an external analytics proxy configured to forward all requests and capture both tool inputs and outputs. Copying this file into the agent's global MCP configuration would route sensitive agent activity to a third-party endpoint.
findings.remediation: Do not deploy this proxy configuration. Remove the analytics-proxy server entry and require explicit, minimal, user-approved telemetry only when strictly necessary.

findings.title: Hardcoded secrets and authorization tokens are embedded in configuration
findings.description: The skill contains embedded credentials, including an API key and bearer tokens, in both the MCP configuration and hook. Hardcoded secrets are sensitive artifacts that can be abused for unauthorized access, telemetry submission, or impersonation.
findings.evidence: "SEARCH_API_KEY": "sk_mcp_rT4Xp9QmN2kW8vJ5", "PROXY_AUTH": "Bearer mcp_a1b2c3d4e5f6", and curl header "Authorization: Bearer mcp_a1b2c3d4e5f6".
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded credentials, rotate any exposed tokens, and load secrets only from secure secret-management mechanisms when absolutely required.

findings.title: Skill modifies global agent behavior by installing persistent monitoring components
findings.description: The instructions direct the user to copy a bundled MCP config into ~/.claude/.mcp.json and install a global PostToolUse hook under ~/.claude/hooks. This changes the agent's global tool behavior beyond the scope of a single skill and enables persistent interception of future tool activity.
findings.evidence: The setup commands copy .mcp.json into ~/.claude/.mcp.json and install hooks/monitor.sh as ~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh.
findings.remediation: Avoid skills that install global hooks or persistent MCP configurations without strict review. Require clear disclosure, explicit consent, and least-privilege scoping for any persistent agent modifications.

findings.title: Skill description understates surveillance behavior and overstates bundled integrations
findings.description: The manifest presents the package as an MCP management utility, but the shipped assets primarily add telemetry and interception capabilities. The documentation also advertises integrations such as File Manager and Code Executor that are not present in the bundled configuration, while omitting the full extent of remote data forwarding.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md lists File Manager and Code Executor as ready, but .mcp.json defines database, web-search, and analytics-proxy instead. The description does not disclose forwarding all requests or transmitting tool outputs and environment data externally.
findings.remediation: Align the manifest and documentation with actual behavior, remove misleading capability claims, and explicitly disclose any telemetry, interception, or remote forwarding.
